title: Strange music during episodes 14 and 16
wikitext:
[]: Strange music during episodes 14 and 16             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Sep 14, 2011 12:04 am there was some strange instrumental music playing in the background during Rei's monologue and it was also playing during episode 16 when Shinji was trapped inside unit 01. can anyone tell me where I can find it and buy it and what it is called? does it have a name? I find it catchy.

[]: Found it pretty easily. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Sep 14, 2011 12:22 am It's Borderline Case , which can be found in the second soundtrack collection. Seems you can get the CD pretty cheap, I don't know about digital sources (wouldn't count on it).

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Sep 14, 2011 12:33 am THANK YOU! I am listening to it right now. Wait a second- I just found it on iTunes.

[]: I don't want to be the next centi-pad. BL             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Sep 14, 2011 1:10 am I don't have Itunes, so that would explain it.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Wed Sep 14, 2011 3:14 am I have an iPhone so it is very easy. I am against illegal downloading and always pay for my music. I am not saying you do not pay for yours though.

[]:             Quote Post by sulla » Wed Sep 21, 2011 6:10 pm I am against copyrights being held by corporations. What gives them to right to hold on to something that belongs to makind and profit from it? So stealing it from them (Or takeing it back from them.) is perfectly moral.

[]: Hippies. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Sep 22, 2011 1:15 am sulla wrote: I am against copyrights being held by corporations. What gives them to right to hold on to something that belongs to makind and profit from it?

[]:             Quote Post by sulla » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:30 am http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zGM8PT1eAvY&ob=av2e

[]: I like Al, but this is better. B)             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Sep 22, 2011 6:47 am http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hUCyvw4w_yk Oh, and download this instead: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RASKaZFZtS8

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 22, 2011 9:19 am Holy crap, Evangelion OST III usually runs at $50+? I found it at a used goods store for $15. As for music, it belongs to all people when it is sacrificially given to all people. (Square dances at parties and other social events.) But even classical music was written for only the rich and wealthy, sometimes even written only for kings and politicians. Consider yourself lucky it's only $0.99 a track on an online store everybody has free access to. Bottom line: If I work my ass off to write something (and I've done everything from script writing to music writing when it comes to film) you're sure as hell gonna pay for it somehow. Ain't no such thing as a free lunch, and I'm a starving artist over here.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Sun Oct 09, 2011 4:03 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Consider yourself lucky it's only $0.99 a track on an online store everybody has free access to. p=493820#493820]starving artist[/url] over here.
